commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 11. "The Lord hath heard, and had mercy on Me, the Lord hath become My helper." Nor did "He suffer His holy One to see corruption" (ver. 10).

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
The Lord. Hebrew points determine, "Lord, hear "But the Greek interpreters agree with the Vulgate, which seems better. (Berthier)
St. Jerome, however, makes this a prayer. "Hear "(Haydock)
